name: Hot-And-Spicy Short Ribs
recipeInstructions:
1. Cut 3 lb Beef short ribs into 1-inch lengths.
2. Peel and mince 4 garlic cloves.
3. Peel and mince 2-inch cubes of ginger.
4. Trim and mince 3 medium scallions.
5. Measure out 2/3 cup soy sauce.
6. Measure out 2 tablespoons sesame oil.
7. Measure out 1 1/2 teaspoons spicy sesame oil.
8. Measure out 1 tablespoon sugar.
9. Measure out 2 tablespoons cider vinegar.
10. Measure out 1 tablespoon mirin (sweet rice wine).
11. Measure out 1/4 teaspoon freshly ground black pepper.
12. Toast the sesame seeds by spreading them over the bottom of a pie tin.
13. Roast at 300°F for 12-to-16 minutes, stirring often until they are golden.
14. Score the meat on each short rib in a crisscross pattern, making the cuts about 3/8-inch apart and cutting almost to the bone.
15. Place the short ribs in a large shallow baking pan.
16. Combine the remaining ingredients (soy sauce, sesame oil, spicy sesame oil, sugar, cider vinegar, mirin, black pepper) and pour the marinade over the meat, tossing well to mix.
17. Cover and refrigerate the ribs for 24 hours, turning them in the marinade several times.
18. Preheat the oven to 325°F.
19. Arrange the ribs on a rack in a shallow roasting pan and brush with some of the marinade.
20. Roast uncovered for 20 minutes.
21. Remove the pan from the oven, turn the ribs, and brush them with the marinade.
22. Raise the oven temperature to 500°F.
23. After the ribs have stood for 10 minutes at room temperature, return them to the oven and roast for 5 minutes.
24. Turn again, baste generously with the marinade, and roast for 3-5 more minutes (about 8 minutes for medium-rare).
25. Brush the ribs with any remaining marinade and serve with fluffy boiled rice and a crisp salad.
Makes 4 to 6 Servings
JEAN ANDERSON
PRODIGY GUEST CHEFS COOKBOOK
